title: glycine dubia
intro: Glycine dubia, also known as sweet clover, is a flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to stimulate the production of vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in blood clotting and bone health. Glycine dubia has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anemia, insomnia, and skin conditions, due to its ability to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Glycine dubia are available in tea, capsule, and tincture forms, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
